data.title: BlackBerry rises after Q2 beat, raises FY26 outlook
data.description:
Shares of BlackBerry (NYSE:BB) rose about 1% premarket on Thursday after second quarter results beat estimates and the company raised its fiscal year 2026 outlook.

The Canadian company's fiscal second quarter, ended Aug. 31, saw non-GAAP EPS of $0.04, while revenue grew about 3% year-over-year to $129.6M. Both top and bottom-line numbers exceeded expectations.

"BlackBerry delivered year-over-year revenue growth and expanded gross margins while reducing operating expenses. This combination ensured strong profitability in the second quarter, beating expectations and achieving a second consecutive quarter of GAAP profitability," said BlackBerry's CEO John Giamatteo.

The company's QNX segment's revenue grew 15.36% year-over-year to $63.1M, while Secure Communications' revenue declined about 9.9% year-over-year to $59.9M.

BlackBerry said $20M was returned to shareholders by buying back about 5 million common shares during the quarter.

Outlook

For the full fiscal year 2026, the company raised its revenue expectations to be between $519 and 541M (midpoint at $530M) versus a consensus of $519.74M, compared to prior estimate range of $508M to $538M (midpoint at $523M).

The company expects non-GAAP basic EPS to be in the range of $0.11 to $0.15 (midpoint at $0.13) versus consensus of $0.09, compared to the prior range of $0.08 to $0.10.

For the fiscal third quarter, BlackBerry expects revenue to be between $132M and $140M (midpoint at $136) versus consensus of $138.04M.

The company anticipates non-GAAP basic EPS to be in the range of $0.02 to $0.04 (midpoint $0.03) versus $0.03 consensus.

data.title: Blackstone considers options for Ancestry.com, including possible IPO - report
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) is reviewing its options for family-history website, including a possible sale or initial public offering, according to a media report on Thursday.

Reuters cited unnamed sources for the news.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: European markets turn red as market sentiment sours
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.15am ET.

London (UKX) -0.23% to 9,229. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.66% to 23,508. Germany’s GfK consumer climate index improved slightly in October.

France (CAC:IND) -0.50% to 7,788, consumer confidence in France held at 87 in September.

Among other European nations, Bank lending to households in the Eurozone rose by 2.5% Y/Y in August.

Producer prices in Sweden dropped by 0.7% Y/Y in August.

The annual growth rate of lending to Swedish households edged up to 2.7% in August.

The Swiss National Bank left its policy rate unchanged at 0% and reaffirmed its readiness to intervene in foreign exchange markets if needed.

Hungary’s current account surplus sharply widened in Q2.

The unemployment rate in Hungary rose to 4.4% in June-August 2025.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) turned 0.20% lower to 552.8, extending the prior session losses. Market sentiment turned cautious as traders weighed the monetary policy outlook particularly from the Fed amid a series of scheduled speeches from officials today. 

The U.S. lowered tariffs on auto imports from the European Union to 15%, retroactive to August 1, 2025, implementing the terms of a trade pact agreed earlier this year.

Gold prices steadied around $3,750 per ounce on Thursday, holding near the record highs at the start of the week.

In the bond market, the yield on the 10-year Treasury held near 4.14% on Thursday after climbing in the prior session.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 2.77%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 6 basis points to 4.73%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Starbucks to close some stores that don't meet its coffeehouse vibe criteria
data.description:
Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) announced on Thursday that the board approved a restructuring plan involving the closure of coffeehouses and the further transformation of the company’s support organization. The changes were noted to be part of the "Back to Starbucks" strategy initiated by CEO Brian Niccol.

"During the review, we identified coffeehouses where we're unable to create the physical environment our customers and partners expect, or where we don't see a path to financial performance, and these locations will be closed," Niccol stated in a letter to employees. Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) also plans to cut about 900 jobs in its support teams and close many open positions.

The Seattle-based company estimates that it will incur approximately $1 billion related to the store closures, support organization transformation, and other restructuring activities, with 90% of the expenses attributable to the North America business. 

Starbucks (SBUX) said it will end the fiscal year with nearly 18,300 total Starbucks locations (company operated and licensed) across the U.S. and Canada. In fiscal year 2026, Starbucks (SBUX) plans to grow the number of coffeehouses it operates as it continues to invest in the business.

Shares of Starbucks (SBUX) were down 0.2% in premarket trading. The restaurant stock is down 7.7% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Quantum Computing slips as it closes $500M private placement
data.description:
Quantum Computing (NASDAQ:QUBT) shares slipped 3.5% in premarket trading on Thursday after it disclosed that its previously authorized $500M private placement of common stock has closed. 

The price placement and sale of 26.867M shares resulted in the gross proceeds of $500M for the company, before expenses. Quantum said it will use the net proceeds for general corporate purposes, including strategic acquisitions.

“This successful $500 million offering, backed by strong support from both new and existing top-tier institutional investors, was priced at a significant premium compared to our four recent offerings,” said Dr. Yuping Huang, CEO and Chairman of the Board of Quantum Computing in a statement. “With this transaction, we have raised approximately $900 million in gross proceeds since November 2024. This additional funding further strengthens our balance sheet and positions us to advance our multi-year growth strategy of accelerating commercialization, pursuing strategic acquisitions, expanding our sales and engineering teams, and enhancing our manufacturing capabilities.”

Shares of the Hoboken, N.J.-based company have risen nearly 30% year-to-date, amid an increase in quantum computing companies.

data.title: C2 Blockchain crosses 500 million DOG Coin milestone
data.description:
• Blockchain (OTCPK:CBLO) Thursday announced that its corporate DOG Coin treasury has officially surpassed 500 million DOG tokens, marking a historic milestone in the company's strategy.
  • With holdings now exceeding 500,000,000 DOG coins, C2 Blockchain stands as the largest institutional DOG holder in the public markets, the company said.

data.title: Cipher Mining stock up amid convertible senior notes offering announcement
data.description:
Cipher Mining (NASDAQ:CIFR) stock was trading higher as the data center developer announced a $800M 0.00% convertible senior notes offering to institutional buyers.

Shares were +3.75% Thursday pre-market to $14.67.

The senior, unsecured obligations mature on October 1, 2031, will not bear regular interest, and the principal amount will not accrete.

Also, the initial purchasers are expected to be granted a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $120M of the notes.

The final terms of the notes, including the initial conversion rate and certain other terms, will be determined at the time of pricing.

The conversions are set to be settled by paying or delivering cash, shares of common stock, or a combination of cash and shares of common stock, at Cipher's election. 

Redemption price for the notes will be equal to the principal amount, plus accrued and unpaid special interest and additional interest, if any.

Net proceeds from the offering are intended to be used to fund the cost of entering into related capped call transactions, finance a portion of the data center construction at Barber Lake, accelerate the build-out of its high-performance computing strategy across the 2.4 GW pipeline, continue to expand the pipeline of development sites, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Stock index futures muted ahead of a slew of macroeconomic releases, Fed speak
data.description:
Stock index futures were muted on Thursday, as investors awaited a slew of macroeconomic releases and Fed speak slated for the day.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.2% and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.14%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.61%.

Wall Street's major indexes ended lower on Wednesday, marking a second day of declines, as struggling U.S. technology stocks weighed on the markets and uncertainty persisted among investors.

“Risk assets have struggled to find their footing over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 posting another modest decline after the AI-driven selloff on Tuesday. Overnight futures have recouped around half those losses, but it does seem that we've paused for breath in recent days. There hasn’t been a major catalyst driving this, but decent housing data and stronger oil prices yesterday led to some doubts about the prospect of rapid rate cuts from the Federal Reserve,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

There is a barrage of economic releases on Thursday. 

The August durable goods orders will come in before the opening bell. Total durable goods orders are seen showing a decrease of 0.5% on the month.

GDP will land at the same time. The final revision for Q2 growth is expected to show no change from 3.3% in the last report.

“The U.S. releases revised second-quarter GDP data. The market consensus is for no change. U.S. growth has been distorted this year—export and inventory data swung wildly between the first and second quarters. Unfortunately, the fiction of annualization assumes that what happens in a quarter, however unique, is continually repeated. This exaggerates first-quarter weakness and second-quarter strength,” UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The initial jobless claims and international trade in goods (advance) will also come in at the same time. Claims are seen at 238K in the latest week and the consensus sees the goods trade gap at $94.4 billion in August versus $103.9 billion in July.

“U.S. initial and continuing jobless claims data will get attention, as the Fed prioritizes weak labor markets over higher inflation,” Donovan said. 

During market hours, the August existing home sales report will land. Sales are expected to stay soft at a 3.95 million rate in August versus 4.01 million in July.

The Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day. 

In terms of Fed speak, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, New York Fed President John Williams, Fed Governor Michael Barr and San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, among others, are all slated to take the stage in different events.
